name: The three ports from the Deba River to the Urola River — circular from Azpeitia
name_original: Los tres puertos des del Río Deba hasta el Río Urola — circular desde Azpeitia
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Azpeitia are set within the scenic Urola Valley, flanked by the Izarraitz mountain range in Spain's Basque Country. The region features lush greenery, the Urola River, and varied terrain suitable for road cyclists. The landscape includes both picturesque valley roads and challenging ascents into the mountains, with some routes incorporating parts of the former Urola railway line, now a greenway. This combination provides diverse road cycling experiences, from well-paved surfaces to routes with significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Azpeitia The most popular road cycling route is The three ports from the Deba River to the Urola River — circular from Azpeitia, a 58.4 miles (94.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain across multiple mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Zarautz Beach – Zumaia loop from Azpeitia, a moderate 37.3 miles (60.0 km) path. This route offers coastal views as it connects Azpeitia to the towns of Zarautz and Zumaia. Local road cyclists also love the Mirandaola Forge and Park – Udana Pass Ascent loop from Lasao, a 47.4 miles (76.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including cultural sites and mountain passes, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Azpeitia is defined by the scenic Urola Valley, the challenging Izarraitz mountain range, and the historic Urola greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Azpeitia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Azpeitia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Azpeitia?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Azpeitia, offering a variety of experiences. These include routes with significant elevation gains and others that are more moderate, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Azpeitia?
A: Road cycling in Azpeitia offers diverse terrain. You'll find picturesque valley roads through the scenic Urola Valley, lush greenery, and the meandering Urola River. For more experienced cyclists, the imposing Izarraitz mountain range provides challenging ascents and panoramic views. Some routes also incorporate parts of the former Urola railway line, now a greenway, offering a unique cycling experience.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Azpeitia?
A: Azpeitia benefits from a temperate climate, making it suitable for road cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Azpeitia?
A: While Azpeitia is known for its varied terrain, including challenging climbs, there are moderate routes available that could be suitable for less experienced cyclists or families. These routes often feature well-paved surfaces and less significant elevation gains, allowing for a more relaxed ride through the Urola Valley. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34038421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach – Zumaia loop from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Azpeitia?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, the Izarraitz mountain range provides routes with significant elevation gains. An example of a difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031436378/the-three-ports-from-the-deba-river-to-the-urola-river-circular-from-azpeitia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three ports from the Deba River to the Urola River — circular from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt;, which features multiple mountain passes. Other challenging options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atagoiti – Mandubia Summit loop from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38298490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Iturburu – Mandubia Summit loop from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Azpeitia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Azpeitia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031436378/the-three-ports-from-the-deba-river-to-the-urola-river-circular-from-azpeitia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three ports from the Deba River to the Urola River — circular from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34038421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach – Zumaia loop from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38282878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirandaola Forge and Park – Udana Pass Ascent loop from Lasao&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural or natural attractions can I explore along road cycling routes in Azpeitia?
A: Azpeitia's road cycling routes often pass by or near significant attractions. You can visit the impressive Sanctuary of Loyola, the birthplace of Saint Ignatius of Loyola, or the Basque Railway Museum, which houses a collection of restored railway vehicles. Natural highlights include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algorri Flysch&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1101604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zumaia Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique geological formations, and the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4503659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarautz Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for road cyclists in Azpeitia?
A: Yes, Azpeitia, with its mix of traditional Basque architecture and modern amenities, offers various parking options within the town. Many routes start directly from Azpeitia, making it convenient to park your vehicle before heading out on your ride.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Azpeitia using public transport?
A: Azpeitia has historical connections to public transport, particularly with the former Urola railway line, which has been converted into a greenway. While the railway itself is no longer active for passenger transport, the town's central location in the Urola Valley suggests accessibility via regional bus services. It's advisable to check local public transport schedules for connections to Azpeitia.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Azpeitia?
A: The road cycling routes around Azpeitia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's varied terrain, from scenic valley roads to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained paths that offer diverse experiences for all levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes around Azpeitia?
A: Yes, Azpeitia offers several long-distance road cycling routes for those looking for extended rides. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031436378/the-three-ports-from-the-deba-river-to-the-urola-river-circular-from-azpeitia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three ports from the Deba River to the Urola River — circular from Azpeitia&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 94 km, providing a substantial journey through the region's landscapes and mountain passes.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Azpeitia and the surrounding towns along the routes feature a delightful mix of traditional Basque architecture and modern amenities. You'll find cafes and establishments where you can stop for refreshments, especially in the larger towns and villages, allowing you to refuel and experience local hospitality during your ride.
